title: Automation partially working
excerpt: We have an Automation that is triggered when a new Item is created.  It adds a list of 22 sub-items as a check list to the new Item.  It was working perfection, then suddenly only 2 of the 22 sub-items is being created w…

posts.author: Louise22 (Louise Morel)
posts.cookedHtml: We have an Automation that is triggered when a new Item is created. It adds a list of 22 sub-items as a check list to the new Item. It was working perfection, then suddenly only 2 of the 22 sub-items is being created with a new Item. I checked the Automation but can’t see anything wrong. I don’t understand why it partially works. Thanks

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
• Is this a single automation with 22 separate “create subitem” actions?

  • Does it fail on every new item now, or only some?

  • Did anything change recently with board permissions, ownership, plan limits, or automation usage?

  • What does the Run history show for the failed runs?

  • Can you post a picture of your automation?

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Louise22 !

The automation is partially failing.

Edit the automation and re-save the subitem template. Then turn the automation off and back on.

If it still only creates some subitems, delete the automation and recreate it from scratch with the same 22 subitems.

This clears any broken state in the automation configuration that causes only part of the subitems to be created.

If you’d like hands-on help or want us to walk through this live, you can book a 1:1 paid 60-minute strategy session with our team here:
Calendly

posts.author: Louise22 (Louise Morel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes, this is a single automation with 22 separate “Create subitem” actions.

It fails on every new item.

Nothing has changed with permissions, ownerships or plan limits.

Run history shows it has failed. It has failed several times, but the Run history is only showing this one time. See below.

image001.png451×680 26.3 KB

Below is the automation. There are 22 subitems, not all of them show on the list.

image002.png500×883 56.3 KB

This is part of a template for when new jobs are created.

Thank you,

posts.author: Louise22 (Louise Morel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Tanvi,

Thanks for your suggestions.

I’ve already tried both, and it still doesn’t work.

Have a great day,

posts.author: edarling85 (Wild 6 | Eric Darling)
posts.cookedHtml:
1. Use the workflow builder (Pro & Enterprise plans)

If you have access, we strongly recommend rebuilding this using the workflow builder. It’s more robust for multi-step processes and allows you to:

  • Add delays between actions

  • Better control how subitems are created

  • Reduce the chance of partial execution

Workflows also support more advanced handling of subitems and step-by-step logic, making them more reliable for larger processes.

posts.author: kevinfantasymedia (Kevin - Fantasy Media LTD)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Louise22

If the current setup isn’t behaving the way you expected, another option is to use a template item and copy its subitems, including the subitem column values, into a newly created item.

You can also make it more flexible by choosing which template item to copy from based on a status change (second template in the screenshot below). That’s possible with Subitem Automations, and it works well for setups with multiple templates.

image3026×1088 170 KB

There’s a free plan available, so it’s easy to test. Hope this helps!

If you need help setting this up or if you have any questions at all, feel free to reach out to hello@fantasymedia.io .

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m with Eric on this—if you have access, definitely use the workflow builder (Pro & Enterprise).

That said, this also feels like something worth raising with monday support. I’ve used automations to create subitems plenty of times and they’re usually very reliable. Creating 22 at once is pretty heavy though, so it’s possible the automation is hitting a limit or tripping over itself and erroring out. The fact that it’s happening within a template adds another layer that could be contributing.
